context.description: Chapter 3, “The Light of Awareness,” introduces a section on the “excited ape” and argues that freedom should be understood through access to information rather than escape from the self. It uses a dwarf-and-giant metaphor to reframe human agency as growth within the conditions that make experience possible. A young man’s decision-making under sexual attraction is used as an illustrative example of limited but real choice.
normalization_notes: Translated visible German headings and prose into English control-language values.
Mapped raw category to nonfiction philosophy and subcategory to philosophy of consciousness.

rows.paragraph_text: Having already discussed the machine, the text reframes free will by asking what “free” should mean within the machine. It contrasts childish escape fantasies with a mature recognition that experience depends on the larger system and then proposes that greater freedom may come from increasing access to information.
rows.text_content: Having already discussed the machine, the text reframes free will by asking what “free” should mean within the machine. It contrasts childish escape fantasies with a mature recognition that experience depends on the larger system and then proposes that greater freedom may come from increasing access to information.

rows.paragraph_text: The author introduces two scenarios to test what information is actually available to a self and uses a young man’s choice under sexual attraction as an example of constrained decision-making. The passage argues that freedom can be described by the amount of self-knowledge and accessible information that a person has.
rows.text_content: The author introduces two scenarios to test what information is actually available to a self and uses a young man’s choice under sexual attraction as an example of constrained decision-making. The passage argues that freedom can be described by the amount of self-knowledge and accessible information that a person has.
